purposes.description: Perform fundamental git operations: init/clone repos, stage files, commit changes, view diffs and status.

primitives.evidence: Basic git commands: git init, git add, git commit, git status, git diff.
Run single git commands and use their output.

purposes.description: Create, switch, delete, and rename branches; merge branches and resolve conflicts.

primitives.evidence: Basic git branch/checkout/merge commands with simple flags.
Multi-step workflow: create branch, make changes, merge back, delete branch.
Feature branch workflow is a 3-step sequential process: branch, commit, merge.

purposes.description: Manage remotes, fetch/pull/push changes, push new branches, sync forks with upstream.

primitives.evidence: Basic git remote, fetch, pull, push commands.
Multi-step sync workflow: add upstream, fetch, merge, push.
Fork sync is a sequential procedure: add remote, fetch, checkout main, merge, push.

purposes.description: View commit history with various filters (author, date, file), search commit messages and code changes, use blame and bisect.

primitives.evidence: Uses git log with multiple flags (--graph, --oneline, --author, --since, --grep, -S), git blame, git bisect.
Run history/search commands and interpret output.

purposes.description: Undo changes at working directory, staging, and commit levels; recover deleted branches using reflog.

primitives.evidence: Basic git restore, git reset, git revert commands.
Must choose correct undo strategy based on where the change is (working dir, staging, committed).

purposes.description: Perform advanced operations: interactive rebase, stashing, cherry-pick, tagging, submodules, and cleaning.

primitives.evidence: Commands with multiple options: git rebase -i, git stash with named saves, git cherry-pick, git tag -a with messages, git submodule, git clean with various flags.
Multi-step operations: stash, switch branch, apply stash; or rebase with conflict resolution.
Must decompose complex workflows into steps and handle conditional branches (e.g., rebase conflict resolution).
